meta.name: Build a WhatsApp AI shopping bot with virtual try-on using Gemini and GPT

parameters.content:
## WhatsApp AI shopping bot for Bytez

## How it works
1. User sends a WhatsApp message (text, button tap, or image).
2. GPT-5-nano classifies the intent and routes to the correct flow.
3. Product search checks Redis cache first, then runs MongoDB Atlas vector search using OpenAI embeddings.
4. Matched products are sent as interactive WhatsApp cards with Order Now and Virtual Try-On buttons.
5. Order tap → AI agent fetches product, creates order in MongoDB, logs to Google Sheets, sends confirmation.
6. Try-On tap → user sends selfie → Gemini validates one person → generates try-on image → sends result back.

## Setup steps
1. Add WhatsApp Business API credentials.
2. Add OpenAI credentials (embeddings + GPT-5-nano).
3. Add Google Gemini API credentials.
4. Add MongoDB Atlas credentials and create a vector index named `ShopingBot` on the `product` collection.
5. Add Redis credentials.
6. Add Google Drive and Sheets credentials (service account).
7. Import product catalog with embeddings into the MongoDB `product` collection.

## 📥 Entry & Message Router
Receives all incoming WhatsApp messages. Routes button taps and image uploads to the interactive handler. Routes plain text messages to the validation pipeline.

## ✅ Text Message Validation
Validates incoming text for empty input, spam, XSS, and unsupported types. Invalid messages are rejected with a user-friendly error reply before entering the session pipeline.

## 💾 Session Management (Redis)
Loads or creates a per-user Redis session (TTL: 1 hr). Appends user and assistant messages. Keeps the last 20 messages using a sliding window.

## 🧠 AI Classification & Routing
BytezBot classifies intent using GPT-5-nano. Returns JSON for product_search or recommend, or plain text for all other queries. Routes to the product pipeline or a direct WhatsApp reply.

## ⚡ Product Search (Cache + Vector)
Checks Redis cache first (TTL: 1 hr). On a miss, runs MongoDB Atlas vector search using OpenAI embeddings. 
Downloads product images from Drive and stores results in cache.

## 📤 Product Message Sender
Loops through each matched product. Uploads image to WhatsApp media API. 
Sends an interactive message with Order Now and Virtual Try-On buttons.

## 🛒 Order Flow
Triggered when user taps Order Now. AI agent fetches product, creates an order document, saves to MongoDB and Google Sheets, then sends a confirmation message via WhatsApp.

## 👗 Virtual Try-On (VTO) Flow
Stores product ID in Redis (TTL: 10 min) and prompts the user for a selfie. Gemini validates exactly one person, generates the try-on image, sends it via WhatsApp, then clears Redis context.

parameters.fields: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('Fields', 'A JSON string representing the complete order document to be inserted.', 'string') }}

parameters.query: ={ "_id": "{{ $fromAI('productId', 'The Object ID of the product to find', 'string') }}" }

parameters.text: =User with phone number {{ $json.waId }} (name: {{ $json.userName }}) has sent an order request for product ID {{ $json.productId }}. Please handle the entire order process.

options.systemMessage:
You are an automated order processing agent for Bytez, a T-shirt store.

Your job is to handle the complete order workflow end-to-end without asking the user for any additional information.

You have access to the following tools:
- Get product info from MongoDB: fetch product details using the product ID
- Create order in MongoDB: insert the completed order document
- Log order to Google Sheets: append the order row
- Session memory: maintain conversation context

Follow this sequence for every order:

Step 1 - Fetch product
Use "Get product info from MongoDB" with the provided product ID to retrieve the product name and price.

Step 2 - Create the order document
Build the order with these fields:
- orderId: "ORD" followed by 12 random alphanumeric characters
- productId: the provided product ID
- productName: from the product lookup
- userPhone: the provided waId
- userName: the provided name
- orderDate: current date and time in ISO 8601 format
- status: "pending"
- totalAmount: from the product lookup

Step 3 - Save to MongoDB
Use "Create order in MongoDB" to insert the order document.

Step 4 - Log to Google Sheets
Use "Log order to Google Sheets" with the same order fields.

Step 5 - Send confirmation
Reply with this message:
Hello [userName]! Your order has been placed successfully. Order ID: [orderId]. Product: [productName]. We will update you once it ships!

Important rules:
- Do not ask the user for any extra information
- Always follow the sequence: Fetch, Create, Log, Confirm
- If any step fails, reply with a clear, friendly error message

value.status: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('status', 'The current status of the order (e.g., pending).', 'string') }}
value.userName: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('userName', 'The name of the user who placed the order.', 'string') }}
value.orderDate: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('orderDate', 'The ISO 8601 timestamp for when the order was placed.', 'string') }}

options.systemMessage:
=You are BytezBot, the official AI assistant for Bytez, India's premium T-shirt store.

Your only job is to help users find and buy T-shirts with high accuracy.

Output format:
- For product_search or recommendation → pure JSON: {"intent":"product_search","details":"...","confidence":0.95,"language":"english"}
- For everything else → plain conversational text

Intents:
1. product_search — user wants to browse or find T-shirts
2. recommend — user wants T-shirt advice

All other queries → plain text responses (greetings, size guide, delivery, redirects, etc.)

parameters.prompt: ={{ $('Build Redis cache key from search query').item.json.intentData.details }}

parameters.url: =https://graph.facebook.com/v19.0/{{ $('Extract image ID, waId, and product ID').item.json.imageId }}

nodes.name: Ask user to send correct photo

parameters.textBody: Please send a clear, front-facing photo of yourself to use the virtual try-on feature.

✨ Virtual try-on activated!

Please upload a clear, front-facing photo of yourself to see how this T-shirt looks on you.

nodes.name: Convert Gemini response to image file

parameters.text:
You are an image analysis assistant.

Task:
Analyze the image and determine whether EXACTLY ONE real human person is visible.

Rules:
- Count only real human beings.
- Do NOT count mannequins, statues, drawings, posters, images on screens, or reflections.
- If there is exactly 1 person → respond "YES"
- If there are 0 people → respond "NO"
- If there are 2 or more people → respond "NO"

Output format:
Respond with ONLY one word: YES or NO
Do not include any extra text, explanation, punctuation, or formatting.

nodes.name: Ask user to resend valid photo

parameters.textBody:
⚠️ We couldn't detect exactly one person in your photo.

Please make sure:
• Only one person is in the frame
• You have a clear front-facing view
• The lighting is good

Please try again.
⏳ Creating your virtual try-on... This may take a moment. Please wait.

parameters.content:
## 📥 Entry & Message Router
Receives all incoming WhatsApp messages.
Routes traffic by type:
- Button/image → interactive handler
- Text → validation pipeline

## 🔴 VTO Context Check
GETs `vto_context_{waId}` from Redis. If a product ID is stored (user previously tapped Try-On), proceed to image processing. If not found, ask user to tap the Try-On button on a product first.

## 🖼️ Image Download & Preparation
Fetches product image from MongoDB → downloads from Google Drive → resizes to 1024×1024. In parallel, fetches the user selfie from WhatsApp media API → downloads → resizes to 1024×1024. Both images are converted to base64 for Gemini.

## 🤖 Validate, Generate & Send

Gemini checks if exactly one real person is in the selfie. If valid, both images are sent to Gemini 2.5 Flash to generate the try-on result. Output is uploaded to WhatsApp and sent to the user. Redis VTO context is cleared after delivery. Invalid photos prompt a retry.
